title: MAS Manifest
description: Schema for Multi-Agent System manifest files (kind: MAS). Defines the agent topology, workflow routing, and optional capabilities for a MAS application.

apiVersion.description: API version of the manifest format.

kind.description: Resource kind. Must be 'MAS' for a multi-agent system manifest.

x-canvas-positions.description: Studio UI layout positions for visual canvas (not used at runtime).

name.description: Unique identifier for the MAS. Used in CLI, benchmark IDs, and directory names.

version.description: Semantic version of this MAS definition.

description.description: Human-readable description of the MAS purpose and capabilities.

default_flavour.description: Name of the default flavour to use when none is specified at runtime.

source.description: Origin of this manifest (e.g., 'openclaw_workspace' for auto-generated manifests).

workspace_root.description: Relative path to the workspace root that generated this manifest.

MASIntent.description: Top-level intent declaration (used by runtime for system prompt context).

summary.description: Natural language summary of what this MAS does. May be injected into the coordinator's system prompt.
Natural language summary of what this MAS does.

Spec.description: Core specification of the MAS: agents, workflow, and optional runtime configuration.

agents.description: List of agents in the system. Each agent is either a ref to an external agent.yaml or an inline definition.

flavour.description: Path to the flavour file for this MAS (alternative to default_flavour in metadata).

entry_agent.description: ID of the entry agent (shorthand alternative to workflow.entry).

tools_ref.description: Path to a shared tools manifest (tools.yaml) used by all agents.

infra_refs.description: List of paths to infrastructure manifests (LLM proxies, tool providers, etc.).

Agency.description: Agent registry (Studio format). Alternative to spec.agents — used when agents are nested under spec.agency.agents.

AgentEntry.description: An agent participating in the MAS. Can be a reference to an external manifest or an inline definition with full agent spec fields.

id.description: Unique identifier for this agent within the MAS. Used in workflow routing and delegation.

ref.description: Relative path to the agent's manifest file (agent.yaml). Resolved relative to the MAS manifest location.

role.description: Role of this agent in the system.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the agent (used in inline definitions).

instructions.description: System prompt / instructions for the agent (inline definition only; typically provided via ref).

delegates_to.description: List of agent IDs this agent can delegate work to.

models.description: LLM model configurations for this agent.

skills.description: Skill names the agent can consult via consult_skills.

intent.description: Natural language description of this agent's purpose.

Workflow.description: Defines how agents interact: entry point, routing topology, and delegation graph.

entry.description: ID of the agent that receives the initial user prompt.

nodes.description: Workflow nodes defining per-agent routing and delegation.

edges.description: Explicit routing edges between agents (Studio format; alternative to delegates_to on nodes).

WorkflowNode.description: A node in the workflow graph. Maps an agent to its delegation targets.

id.description: Identifier matching an agent ID in spec.agents.

agent.description: Agent ID this node routes to (when node ID differs from agent ID).

delegates_to.description: List of agent IDs this node can delegate to.

WorkflowEdge.description: An explicit directed edge in the workflow graph (Studio visual format).

to.description: Target agent ID or list of target agent IDs.

label.description: Optional label for the edge (e.g., condition or description).

path.description: File path for the events.jsonl telemetry output.

MemoryStores.description: References to memory store files for long-term agent memory.

episodic_ref.description: Path to episodic memory store (conversation history, past interactions).

semantic_ref.description: Path to semantic memory store (facts, knowledge base).

procedural_ref.description: Path to procedural memory store (learned procedures, skills).
